debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
β : Type u_2
G : Graph α β
M : Set β
hM : @Graph.IsMatching α β G M
⊢ M.encard ≤ @Graph.MatchingNumber α β G

proof:
have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ (M : Set β), G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> rfl
  
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ (M : Set β), G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Show that the set is bounded above
      have h₃ : BddAbove {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ (M : Set β), G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
        -- Since the set is a subset of ℕ∞, it is bounded above by ⊤
        refine' ⟨⊤, _⟩
        intro n hn
        -- Every element in the set is ≤ ⊤
        simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hn
        rcases hn with ⟨M, hM, rfl⟩
        simp [le_top]
      exact h₃
    · -- Show that M.encard is in the set
      exact h₁
  
  have h₃ : M.encard ≤ ν(G) := by
    have h₄ : ν(G) = sSup {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ (M : Set β), G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
      rfl
    rw [h₄] at *
    exact h₂
  
  exact h₃
failed_attempts:
have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  have h₂ : ∀ (n : ℕ∞), n ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} → n ≤ ν(G) := by
    intro n hn
    apply csSup_le
    · -- Show that the set is nonempty
      -- This is true because we have at least the empty matching
      refine' ⟨0, _⟩
      -- There exists a matching with encard 0 (empty matching)
      use ∅
      refine' ⟨by simp [Set.subset_univ], _, ⟨by simp, by simp⟩⟩
      <;> simp_all [Set.disjoint_left]
    · -- Show that the set is bounded above
      exact hn
  exact h₂ (M.encard) h₁

have h : ∃ (M' : Set β), G.IsMatching M' ∧ M'.encard = M.encard := ⟨M, hM, rfl⟩
  have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, _⟩
    exact ⟨hM, rfl⟩
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Show that the set is bounded above
      use (M.encard : ℕ∞)
      intro x hx
      -- For any x in the set, x ≤ M.encard
      rcases hx with ⟨M', hM', rfl⟩
      -- This step needs to show that M'.encard ≤ M.encard, but we don't have that information.
      -- This suggests that the set is not necessarily bounded above, which is a contradiction.
      -- However, we know that the matching number is the supremum of the set of matching sizes, so we need to rethink our approach.
      -- We should instead note that the set contains all possible matching sizes, so it is unbounded unless there is a maximum.
      -- But since we are working with ℕ∞, which includes ∞, the supremum exists.
      -- So we should use the fact that M'.encard is a member of ℕ∞ and is therefore bounded by itself.
      simp_all [le_refl]
    · -- Show that M.encard is in the set
      exact h₁
  -- Since ν(G) is the supremum of the set of matching sizes, M.encard ≤ ν(G)
  simpa [MatchingNumber] using h₂

have h : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  have h₁ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Prove that the set is bounded above
      use ∞
      intro x hx
      rcases hx with ⟨M', hM', rfl⟩
      have h₂ : (M' : Set β).encard ≤ ∞ := by simp
      exact h₂
    · -- Prove that M.encard is in the set
      exact h
  simpa [MatchingNumber] using h₁

have h : ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ M.encard = M.encard := ⟨M, hM, rfl⟩
  have h₁ : M.encard ≤ ν(G) := by
    apply le_csSup
    · exact ⟨0, by
        simp [MatchingNumber]
        <;>
        (try decide) <;>
        (try
          {
            use ∅
            simp [IsMatching, Set.Subset.refl, Disjoint]
            <;>
            aesop
          })
        ⟩
    · simp [MatchingNumber]
      <;>
      (try decide) <;>
      (try
        {
          use M
          <;>
          aesop
        })
  exact h₁

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup (by
      -- Prove that the set is bounded above
      use (M.encard + 1 : ℕ∞)
      intro n hn
      rcases hn with ⟨M', hM', rfl⟩
      have h₃ : M'.encard ≤ M'.encard := le_rfl
      have h₄ : M'.encard + 1 > M'.encard := by
        simp [Nat.lt_add_one_iff]
        <;> exact lt_succ_self _
      -- Use the fact that M'.encard is a natural number to conclude the proof
      simp_all [Nat.lt_add_one_iff]
      <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try simp_all [Nat.lt_add_one_iff]) <;>
      (try linarith) <;>
      (try omega)
      ) h₁
  simpa [MatchingNumber] using h₂

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Show that the set is bounded above (e.g., by ⊤)
      refine' ⟨⊤, _⟩
      intro n hn
      simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hn
      rcases hn with ⟨M', hM', rfl⟩
      simp [ENat.le_top]
    · -- Show that M.encard is in the set
      exact h₁
  simpa [MatchingNumber] using h₂

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Prove that the set is bounded above
      use ν(G)
      <;> simp [MatchingNumber]
      <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try aesop)
    · -- Prove that M.encard is in the set
      exact h₁
  -- Since ν(G) is the supremum of the set, M.encard ≤ ν(G)
  simpa [MatchingNumber] using h₂

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  -- Since M.encard is an element of the set, it is less than or equal to the supremum of the set.
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Show that the set is bounded above
      refine' ⟨0, _⟩
      intro x hx
      obtain ⟨M', hM', rfl⟩ := hx
      -- Use the fact that the cardinality of any set of edges is non-negative
      simp [hM'.subset]
      <;>
      aesop
    · -- Show that M.encard is in the set
      exact h₁
  -- The supremum of the set is the matching number of G
  simp [MatchingNumber] at h₂ ⊢
  <;>
  exact h₂

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> rfl
  
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Prove that the set is bounded above
      use (M.encard + 1 : ℕ∞)
      intro n hn
      rcases hn with ⟨M', hM', rfl⟩
      have h₃ : (M'.encard : ℕ∞) < (M'.encard + 1 : ℕ∞) := by
        cases M'.encard <;> simp_all [Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_one, add_assoc]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> simp_all [lt_add_iff_pos_right]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> omega
      have h₄ : (M'.encard : ℕ∞) ≤ (M'.encard + 1 : ℕ∞) := by
        exact le_of_lt h₃
      exact h₄
    · -- Prove that M.encard is in the set
      exact h₁
  
  have h₃ : M.encard ≤ ν(G) := by
    simpa [MatchingNumber] using h₂
  
  exact h₃

have h : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M', G.IsMatching M' ∧ n = M'.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  
  have h₁ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M', G.IsMatching M' ∧ n = M'.encard} := by
    have h₂ : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M', G.IsMatching M' ∧ n = M'.encard} := h
    exact le_csSup (by
      -- Show that the set is bounded above
      have h₃ : BddAbove {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M', G.IsMatching M' ∧ n = M'.encard} := by
        -- The cardinality of the edge set of G is an upper bound
        use (E G).encard
        intro n hn
        rcases hn with ⟨M', hM', rfl⟩
        have h₄ : M' ⊆ E G := hM'.subset
        exact encard_le_of_subset h₄
      exact h₃
    ) h₂
  
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ ν(G) := by
    have h₃ : ν(G) = sSup {n | ∃ M', G.IsMatching M' ∧ n = M'.encard} := rfl
    rw [h₃] at *
    exact h₁
  
  exact h₂

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Show that the set is bounded above
      use 0
      intro n hn
      -- This is a placeholder for the actual bound. In reality, we need to find an actual upper bound for the set.
      -- For simplicity, we assume the set is bounded above by 0.
      simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try {
        have h₃ := hn.2
        simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try aesop)
      }) <;>
      (try {
        exfalso
        have h₃ := hn.2
        simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try aesop)
      })
      <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try aesop)
      })
    · -- Show that the element is in the set
      exact h₁
  -- Simplify the right-hand side to match the definition of MatchingNumber
  have h₃ : sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} = ν(G) := by
    rfl
  -- Use the fact that the element is less than or equal to the supremum to complete the proof
  rw [h₃] at h₂
  exact h₂

  ) _)
  -- Show that M.encard is in the set {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard}

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Prove that the set is nonempty and bounded above
      have h₃ : {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard}.BddAbove := by
        use (E(G) : Set β).encard
        rintro n ⟨M', hM', rfl⟩
        have h₄ : M' ⊆ E(G) := hM'.subset
        have h₅ : M'.encard ≤ (E(G) : Set β).encard := by
          apply encard_mono
          exact h₄
        exact h₅
      have h₄ : {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard}.Nonempty := by
        refine' ⟨M.encard, _⟩
        exact ⟨M, hM, rfl⟩
      exact by
        simp_all [Set.Nonempty]
        <;>
        aesop
    · -- Prove that M.encard is in the set
      exact h₁
  -- Conclude the proof by noting that ν(G) is the supremum of the set

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by

        refine' ⟨M.encard, _⟩
        exact h₁
      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := h₃
      refine' ⟨n, _⟩
      aesop
    · -- Show that M.encard is less than or equal to every element in the set
      intro n hn
      simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hn
      obtain ⟨M', hM', hn'⟩ := hn
      rw [hn'] at *
      <;> simp_all [hM'.subset]
      <;> aesop
  simpa [MatchingNumber] using h₂

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    rfl
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Show that the set is bounded above
      refine' ⟨ν(G), _⟩
      intro n hn
      rcases hn with ⟨M', hM', rfl⟩
      -- Since ν(G) is the supremum of the set of cardinalities of matchings, it is greater than or equal to any such cardinality
      have h₃ : M'.encard ≤ ν(G) := by
        -- This follows directly from the definition of ν(G)
        simp [ν]
        <;>
        simp_all [IsMatching]
        <;>
        aesop
      exact h₃
    · -- Show that M.encard is in the set
      exact h₁
  -- Since ν(G) is defined as the supremum of the set of cardinalities of matchings, we have M.encard ≤ ν(G)
  simpa [ν] using h₂

    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by

      intro x hx
      have h₃ : x ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := hx
      have h₄ : x ≤ sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
        apply le_csSup
        · -- Show that the set is bounded above
          exact ⟨sSup {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard}, by
            intro y hy
            have h₅ : y ∈ {n | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := hy
            simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at h₅
            -- Since y is in the set, it is trivially less than or equal to the supremum

        · -- Show that x is in the set

      exact h₄
    · -- Show that M.encard is in the set
      exact h
  -- Since ν(G) is the supremum of the set, we have M.encard ≤ ν(G)
  simpa [MatchingNumber] using h₂

have h₁ : ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ M.encard = M.encard := ⟨M, hM, rfl⟩
  have h₂ : M.encard ∈ {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := ⟨M, hM, rfl⟩

      exact le_csSup (by

        have h₄ : ∃ (M : Set β), G.IsMatching M := ⟨∅, by

          simp [Graph.IsMatching.disjoint]

          ⟩
        -- The set of possible M.encard values is non-empty

        -- The set is bounded above by the supremum

          -- Since all M.encard are bounded by some number, we can use that
          -- This is a placeholder for a more rigorous proof
          use ⊤
          intro n hn
          simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hn
          obtain ⟨M, hM, rfl⟩ := hn
          exact le_top
        -- The set is bounded above
        exact h₆) hn
    · -- Show that the element is in the set
      exact h₂
  -- The result follows from the definition of ν(G)
  simpa [ν] using h₃

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M : Set β, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  -- Use the fact that the supremum of a set containing M.encard is at least M.encard
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M : Set β, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Show that the set is bounded above

apply le_csSup
  · -- Prove that the set is bounded above
    refine' ⟨⊤, _⟩
    -- We need to show that every element in the set is ≤ ⊤, which is always true
    intro n hn
    -- Extract the property from the set definition
    rcases hn with ⟨M', hM', rfl⟩
    -- Since M'.encard is a cardinal number, it is ≤ ⊤

  · -- Prove that M.encard is in the set
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    -- We need to show that M.encard = M.encard, which is trivially true

      intro n hn
      -- Every element in the set is at most ⊤.

    exact h₁) h

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ (M : Set β), G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  exact le_csSup (by
    have h₂ : ∃ (n : ℕ∞), ∀ (a : ℕ∞), a ∈ {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ (M : Set β), G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} → a ≤ n := by
      use (E(G)).encard
      intro a ha
      rcases ha with ⟨M, hM', rfl⟩
      have h₃ : M ⊆ E(G) := hM'.subset
      exact encard_le_encard h₃
    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := h₂
    refine' ⟨n, _⟩
    intro a ha
    exact hn a ha) h₁

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M : Set β, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  -- Use the fact that the supremum of the set is an upper bound for every element in the set
  exact le_sSup h₁

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  have h₂ : BddAbove {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨⊤, _⟩
    intro n hn
    simp_all [le_top]
  have h₃ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup h₂
    exact h₁
  simpa [MatchingNumber] using h₃

have h : M.encard ∈ {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M : Set β, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ ν(G) := by
    have h₃ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M : Set β, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
      apply le_csSup
      · -- Show that the set is bounded above
        use ⊤
        intro x hx
        simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hx
        rcases hx with ⟨M, hM, rfl⟩
        -- Since M.encard is a cardinality, it is less than or equal to ⊤
        exact by simp [le_top]
      · -- Show that M.encard is in the set
        exact h
    -- By definition, ν(G) is the supremum of the set
    simpa [MatchingNumber] using h₃
  
  exact h₂

have h₂ : M.encard ∈ {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  have h₃ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ n = M.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Prove that the set is bounded above
      use ⊤
      intro n hn
      obtain ⟨M', hM', rfl⟩ := hn
      simp_all [le_top]
    · -- Prove that M.encard is in the set
      exact h₂
  simp_all [MatchingNumber]
  <;>
  (try aesop)

have h₁ : M.encard ∈ {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ (M' : Set β), G.IsMatching M' ∧ n = M'.encard} := by
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    <;> simp
  
  have h₂ : M.encard ≤ sSup {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ (M' : Set β), G.IsMatching M' ∧ n = M'.encard} := by
    apply le_csSup
    · -- Show that the set is bounded above
      use ⊤
      intro x hx
      -- Every element in the set is less than or equal to ⊤
      simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hx
      rcases hx with ⟨M', hM', rfl⟩
      -- The encoded cardinality of any set is less than or equal to ⊤
      exact le_top
    · -- Show that M.encard is in the set
      exact h₁
  
  have h₃ : sSup {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ (M' : Set β), G.IsMatching M' ∧ n = M'.encard} = ν(G) := by
    rfl
  
  have h₄ : M.encard ≤ ν(G) := by
    calc
      M.encard ≤ sSup {n : ℕ∞ | ∃ (M' : Set β), G.IsMatching M' ∧ n = M'.encard} := h₂
      _ = ν(G) := by rw [h₃]
  
  exact h₄
